title: 凌晨便利店的暖光
coverQuote: At 2am the convenience store light spills onto the wet pavement. A small rectangle of warmth in a dark city.

sentences.en: The automatic door slides open. The cashier looks up for half a second, then back down.
sentences.zh: 自动门滑开。店员抬了半秒头，又低下去。

sentences.en: I walk past the rice balls and the sandwiches. Everything looks the same as yesterday.
sentences.zh: 我走过饭团和三明治。每样都和昨天一样。

sentences.en: At 2am the convenience store light spills onto the wet pavement. A small rectangle of warmth in a dark city.
sentences.zh: 凌晨两点，便利店的光洒在湿路面上。黑暗城市里一小块长方形的暖。

sentences.en: I pick a can of hot milk tea from the warmer. The can is slightly dented near the bottom.
sentences.zh: 我从热饮柜拿了一罐热奶茶。罐底有点凹了。

sentences.en: I stand by the window counter, peeling the label off the can with my thumbnail.
sentences.zh: 我靠着窗台站着，用指甲一点点撕掉罐子上的标签。

sentences.en: A motorcycle goes past outside. The sound fades into the hum of the fridge.
sentences.zh: 外面一辆摩托车经过。声音渐渐融入冰箱的嗡鸣。

title: 末班地铁之后
coverQuote: After the last train leaves, the platform goes quiet. Not empty. Just quiet.

sentences.en: The last train pulls out of the station at 11:52. The platform announcements stop.
sentences.zh: 末班车十一点五十二驶出站台。广播声停了。

sentences.en: A cleaner pushes a wide mop across the floor. The wet trail gleams under the fluorescent lights for ten seconds before it dries.
sentences.zh: 清洁工推着大拖把拖地。湿痕在荧光灯下闪了十秒，然后干了。

sentences.en: After the last train leaves, the platform goes quiet. Not empty. Just quiet.
sentences.zh: 末班车走后，站台安静了。不是空了。只是安静了。

sentences.en: I sit on the metal bench. It is cold through my jeans.
sentences.zh: 我坐在金属长椅上。凉意透过牛仔裤传上来。

sentences.en: A man in a blue uniform walks past, jingling a set of keys. He nods at me without breaking stride.
sentences.zh: 一个穿蓝色工服的男人走过去，手里的钥匙叮当响。他没停脚，冲我点了点头。

sentences.en: I check my phone. No messages. I put it back in my pocket and sit for another three minutes.
sentences.zh: 我看了看手机。没有消息。我又放回口袋，多坐了三分钟。

title: 深夜厨房喝水
coverQuote: I pour a glass of water at 1am. The kitchen is dark except for the blue light from the water dispenser.

sentences.en: I wake up thirsty at 1am. The apartment is completely still.
sentences.zh: 凌晨一点渴醒了。公寓里完全静止。

sentences.en: I walk to the kitchen without turning on any lights. My feet know the way.
sentences.zh: 我摸黑走到厨房。我的脚认识路。

sentences.en: The water dispenser has a small blue light. It is the only thing glowing in the room.
sentences.zh: 饮水机有一小盏蓝灯。是房间里唯一发光的东西。

sentences.en: I pour a glass of water and drink it standing up. The water is cold enough to feel it go down.
sentences.zh: 我倒了一杯水，站着喝完。水凉得能感觉到它一路下去。

sentences.en: I rinse the glass and leave it on the drying rack. Then I go back to bed.
sentences.zh: 我冲了冲杯子，放在沥水架上。然后回到床上。

title: 夜里十点的小区
coverQuote: After 10pm, the neighborhood belongs to dog walkers, insomniacs, and people who just need to get out.

sentences.en: I put on my jacket and step outside. The air is cooler than I expected.
sentences.zh: 我穿上外套走出去。空气比我想的凉。

sentences.en: After 10pm, the neighborhood belongs to a different set of people. Dog walkers. Insomniacs. People who just need to get out.
sentences.zh: 十点以后，这个小区属于另一群人。遛狗的。睡不着的。只是需要出门透口气的人。

sentences.en: An old woman sits on a bench with a small radio. It is playing a song from the 1980s.
sentences.zh: 一个老太太坐在长椅上，旁边放着个小收音机。正在放一首八十年代的歌。

sentences.en: I walk past the closed fruit shop. The metal shutter is pulled down but a light is still on inside.
sentences.zh: 我走过已经关门的水果店。卷帘门拉下来了，但里面还亮着一盏灯。

sentences.en: A cat crosses the path ahead of me. It stops, looks at me for three seconds, then continues.
sentences.zh: 一只猫在我前面穿过小路。它停下来，看了我三秒，然后继续走。

sentences.en: I walk one full loop around the block. By the time I get back, the old woman and her radio are gone.
sentences.zh: 我绕着小区走了一整圈。等我回来，老太太和她的收音机都不在了。

title: 夜班公交司机
coverQuote: The night bus driver has the kind of face that has seen everything and decided not to talk about it.

sentences.en: I get on the N30 night bus. The driver does not look at me. He just waits for the beep of my card.
sentences.zh: 我上了N30夜班车。司机没看我。他只等刷卡的那一声"嘀"。

sentences.en: His hands on the steering wheel are steady. The nails are clean. A wedding ring on his left hand.
sentences.zh: 他握着方向盘的手很稳。指甲是干净的。左手戴着一个婚戒。

sentences.en: The night bus driver has the kind of face that has seen everything and decided not to talk about it.
sentences.zh: 夜班公交司机有一张什么都见过但选择不说的脸。

sentences.en: There is a thermos in the cup holder next to his seat. The lid is stained brown from years of tea.
sentences.zh: 他座位旁的杯架里有一个保温杯。杯盖被多年的茶水染成了棕色。

sentences.en: At each stop he slows down, checks the mirror, opens the door. No one gets on. He closes the door and drives on.
sentences.zh: 每一站他都减速，看后视镜，开门。没人上车。他关上门继续开。

sentences.en: When I get off, I say thank you. He nods once. The doors close behind me and the bus disappears into the dark.
sentences.zh: 我下车时说了声谢谢。他点了一下头。车门在我身后关上，公交车消失在黑暗中。

title: 晚上十一点的阳台
coverQuote: Standing on a balcony at night is the closest you can get to being outside without actually leaving.

sentences.en: I step onto the balcony in my socks. The concrete is dry and cool.
sentences.zh: 我穿着袜子走到阳台上。水泥地又干又凉。

sentences.en: The buildings across the street have about twenty lit windows. Each one is a different shade of yellow.
sentences.zh: 对面楼大概有二十扇亮着灯的窗户。每一扇都是不同色调的黄。

sentences.en: Standing on a balcony at night is the closest you can get to being outside without actually leaving.
sentences.zh: 夜里站在阳台上，是最接近出门但又没真的出门的事。

sentences.en: Someone three floors down is cooking. I can smell garlic and oil.
sentences.zh: 楼下三层有人在做饭。我能闻到蒜和油的味道。

sentences.en: I lean on the railing and watch a taxi turn the corner. Its headlights sweep across the building wall.
sentences.zh: 我靠在栏杆上，看着一辆出租车拐过街角。车灯扫过楼面。

sentences.en: After five minutes my feet get cold. I go back inside and close the sliding door.
sentences.zh: 五分钟后脚冷了。我回到屋里，拉上了推拉门。

title: 失眠人看见的凌晨
coverQuote: At 3:47am a single car passes outside. Its headlights sweep across my ceiling in a slow arc. Then darkness.

sentences.en: The ceiling is a blank screen I have been watching for two hours. I know every crack, every water stain.
sentences.zh: 天花板是一面我看了两个小时的空白屏幕。我认识每一条裂缝，每一个水渍。

sentences.en: At 3:47am a single car passes outside. Its headlights sweep across my ceiling in a slow arc. Then darkness again.
sentences.zh: 凌晨三点四十七分，外面一辆车经过。车灯在天花板上扫出一个缓慢的弧线。然后又暗了。

sentences.en: I turn onto my side. The pillow is warm on one side and cool on the other. I flip it over.
sentences.zh: 我翻了个身。枕头一面是暖的，一面是凉的。我把它翻了过来。

sentences.en: The fridge compressor kicks on in the kitchen. A sound I never notice during the day. At 4am it is a symphony.
sentences.zh: 厨房里的冰箱压缩机启动了。白天从来不会注意到的声音。凌晨四点它成了交响乐。

sentences.en: I think about an email I forgot to send three days ago. Then I think about a conversation from five years ago. 4am does that.
sentences.zh: 我想起三天前忘记发的一封邮件。然后想起五年前的一段对话。凌晨四点就会这样。

sentences.en: Somewhere around 5am I stop trying. If sleep wants to come it knows where I live.
sentences.zh: 大概五点左右我不再挣扎了。如果睡眠想来，它知道我住哪。

title: 楼下便利店店员
coverQuote: The night shift cashier at my convenience store has worked there for two years. I do not know her name.

sentences.en: The night shift cashier always wears the same green apron. It is faded around the pockets.
sentences.zh: 夜班店员总穿着同一件绿色围裙。口袋周围已经褪色了。

sentences.en: She scans my items without looking at me. Not rude. Just efficient. The scanner beeps three times.
sentences.zh: 她扫码时不看我。不是没礼貌。就是效率很高。扫码枪响了三声。

sentences.en: The night shift cashier at my convenience store has worked there for two years. I do not know her name.
sentences.zh: 楼下便利店夜班店员已经在那工作了两年。我不知道她的名字。

sentences.en: Once I saw her reading a thick novel behind the counter. The cover was worn. She looked up when the door chimed, then went back to her page.
sentences.zh: 有一次我看到她在柜台后面读一本很厚的小说。封面被翻烂了。门铃响了她抬头看了一眼，又继续看。

sentences.en: I wonder if she notices when I do not show up. Probably not. But I like that we have this thing.
sentences.zh: 我不知道我不去的时候她会不会注意到。大概不会。但我喜欢我们有这种默契。

sentences.en: Tonight I buy the usual: a rice ball and a can of tea. She scans them. I pay. We do not speak. It is perfect.
sentences.zh: 今晚我照常买了：一个饭团，一罐茶。她扫码。我付款。我们没说话。这样很好。

title: 末班公交上的四个人
coverQuote: Four strangers on the last bus, each in a separate pool of yellow light from the reading lamps above.

sentences.en: The last bus has four passengers. An old man with a plastic bag of vegetables. A woman in a nurse uniform. A teenager with headphones. Me.
sentences.zh: 末班车上有四个乘客。一个拎着塑料袋蔬菜的老人。一个穿护士服的女人。一个戴耳机的少年。我。

sentences.en: Each of us sits under our own reading lamp, creating four separate pools of yellow light along the aisle.
sentences.zh: 我们各自坐在自己的阅读灯下，沿着过道形成了四个互不相连的黄色光圈。

sentences.en: The nurse is asleep with her head against the window. Her shoes are off under the seat.
sentences.zh: 护士靠着车窗睡着了。鞋子脱在座位下面。

sentences.en: The old man counts his vegetables. Potatoes. Green onions. A single tomato wrapped in newspaper.
sentences.zh: 老人在数他的菜。土豆。小葱。一个用报纸包着的番茄。

sentences.en: The teenager stares at his phone. The screen light flickers blue on his face. He smiles at something.
sentences.zh: 少年盯着手机。屏幕的蓝光在他脸上闪烁。他看到什么笑了一下。

sentences.en: The bus stops. The nurse wakes up, puts on her shoes, and gets off. Now there are three pools of light.
sentences.zh: 车到站了。护士醒了，穿上鞋，下了车。现在只剩三个光圈了。

title: 夜里回家闻到别人家的饭
coverQuote: Walking home at 9pm, I pass six apartments. Each one leaks a different dinner smell through the gaps under the doors.

sentences.en: I walk up the stairs to my apartment on the sixth floor. The elevator is broken again.
sentences.zh: 我走楼梯上六楼的公寓。电梯又坏了。

sentences.en: Walking home at 9pm, I pass six apartments. Each one leaks a different dinner smell through the gaps under the doors.
sentences.zh: 晚上九点走路回家，我经过六户人家。每家都从门缝底下漏出不同的晚饭味道。

sentences.en: Third floor: something frying. Garlic and soy sauce. Fourth floor: boiled vegetables. Fifth floor: instant noodles.
sentences.zh: 三楼：在炒东西。蒜和酱油。四楼：煮蔬菜。五楼：泡面。

sentences.en: My apartment has no dinner smell waiting. Just the faint scent of laundry detergent from this morning.
sentences.zh: 我的公寓没有晚饭味在等我。只有早上洗衣液留下的淡淡味道。

sentences.en: I unlock the door, drop my bag by the shoe rack, and stand in the dark hallway for a full minute before turning on the light.
sentences.zh: 我打开门，把包丢在鞋架旁，在黑暗的玄关站了整整一分钟才开灯。

sentences.en: Then I open my fridge and see what I have. Half an onion. Two eggs. That will do.
sentences.zh: 然后我打开冰箱看看有什么。半个洋葱。两个鸡蛋。够了。

title: 关灯后的房间
coverQuote: After I turn off the lights, the room doesn't go completely dark. The streetlight outside paints a thin orange stripe on the wall.

sentences.en: I turn off the desk lamp. The room does not go completely dark.
sentences.zh: 我关了台灯。房间没有完全变黑。

sentences.en: The streetlight outside my window paints a thin orange stripe on the wall above my bed.
sentences.zh: 窗外的路灯在床头上方的墙上画了一道细细的橙色条纹。

sentences.en: I lie on my back and watch the stripe. It does not move. It is just there, keeping me company.
sentences.zh: 我仰面躺着，看着那道条纹。它不动。它就在那儿，陪着我。

sentences.en: I can hear the neighbor's TV through the wall. A laugh track. Then another.
sentences.zh: 能隔着墙听到邻居的电视声。一阵笑声罐头。又一阵。

sentences.en: The orange stripe will be there every night. I have never thanked it.
sentences.zh: 那道橙色条纹每晚都在。我从来没谢过它。

sentences.en: I close my eyes. The stripe is still there behind my eyelids, a warm orange glow.
sentences.zh: 我闭上眼睛。那道条纹还在眼皮后面，一片暖橙色的光。

title: 深夜洗衣房
coverQuote: The laundromat at midnight. Just me and the rhythmic thump of someone else's clothes in the dryer.

sentences.en: I forgot to do laundry during the day so here I am at midnight, pouring detergent into the cap.
sentences.zh: 我白天忘了洗衣服，所以半夜在这里，把洗衣液倒进瓶盖里。

sentences.en: The washing machine starts its cycle with a low hum. Water rushing in. Then the drum begins to turn.
sentences.zh: 洗衣机开始运转，发出低沉的嗡鸣。水灌进去。然后滚筒开始转。

sentences.en: The laundromat at midnight. Just me and the rhythmic thump of someone else's clothes in the dryer.
sentences.zh: 深夜洗衣房。只有我和烘干机里别人衣服有节奏的拍打声。

sentences.en: I sit on the plastic chair and open my phone. No signal in the basement. So I just watch the machines.
sentences.zh: 我坐在塑料椅子上打开手机。地下室里没信号。所以我只是看着机器。

sentences.en: The dryer next to mine is empty. Its door is slightly open. A single forgotten sock is stuck to the drum.
sentences.zh: 我旁边的烘干机是空的。门半开着。一只被遗忘的袜子粘在滚筒上。

sentences.en: The spin cycle ends. Silence for three seconds. Then the rinse water drains out with a gurgle.
sentences.zh: 甩干结束了。安静了三秒。然后漂洗水咕噜咕噜地排出去。

title: 雨打在咖啡馆天窗上
coverQuote: Rain on the skylight sounds different than rain on a window. Softer. Higher up. Like the sky is tapping to come in.

sentences.en: The coffee shop I go to has a small skylight above the back corner. Most people do not notice it.
sentences.zh: 我去的那家咖啡店后角上方有一个小天窗。大多数人注意不到。

sentences.en: Today it is raining. The drops hit the glass in an irregular pattern, like someone who does not know how to type.
sentences.zh: 今天在下雨。雨滴以不规则的节奏打在玻璃上，像一个不会打字的人在敲键盘。

sentences.en: Rain on the skylight sounds different than rain on a window. Softer. Higher up. Like the sky is tapping to come in.
sentences.zh: 雨打在天窗上的声音和打在窗户上不一样。更轻。更高。像天空在轻轻敲门想进来。

sentences.en: I order a flat white and take the corner seat. The barista knows my order but still asks every time.
sentences.zh: 我点了一杯澳白，坐了角落位置。咖啡师知道我要什么，但每次都还是问一遍。

sentences.en: A woman at the next table is sketching in a small notebook. Her pencil moves in quick short strokes, like the rain above.
sentences.zh: 隔壁桌的女人在一个小本子上画画。她的铅笔快速短促地动着，像头顶的雨。

sentences.en: I stay until the rain stops. The sudden silence after rain is its own kind of sound.
sentences.zh: 我一直待到雨停。雨后的突然安静，本身也是一种声音。

title: 公交车站等雨停
coverQuote: The bus shelter roof leaks in one spot. A single drip lands on the same cracked tile every three seconds.

sentences.en: The rain started while I was halfway to the bus stop. Not a drizzle. The serious kind.
sentences.zh: 我走到半路雨就下起来了。不是毛毛雨。是认真的那种。

sentences.en: I duck under the bus shelter. Three other people are already there. We arrange ourselves at equal distances.
sentences.zh: 我钻进公交站的遮雨棚。已经有三个人在里面了。我们以等距排列好自己。

sentences.en: The bus shelter roof leaks in one spot. A single drip lands on the same cracked tile every three seconds.
sentences.zh: 公交站遮雨棚有一个地方漏水。一滴水每三秒落在同一块裂开的瓷砖上。

sentences.en: A kid in a yellow raincoat jumps in a puddle nearby. His mother pulls him back by the hood.
sentences.zh: 一个穿黄色雨衣的小孩在旁边跳进水坑里。他妈妈揪着帽子把他拉回来。

sentences.en: The bus arrives seven minutes late. We board in single file, shaking off umbrellas and jacket sleeves.
sentences.zh: 公交车晚了七分钟到。我们排成一列上车，抖着雨伞和袖子。

sentences.en: I find a window seat. The glass is fogged. I wipe a circle with my palm and watch the wet city slide past.
sentences.zh: 我找到一个窗边的位置。玻璃上都是雾。我用手掌擦出一个圆圈，看着湿漉漉的城市滑过去。

title: 下雨的星期一下午
coverQuote: A rainy Monday afternoon is the world's way of telling you to slow down. The traffic outside agrees.

sentences.en: It started raining at 2pm and has not stopped. The sky is a solid sheet of gray.
sentences.zh: 下午两点开始下雨，一直没停。天空是一整块灰色。

sentences.en: A rainy Monday afternoon is the world's way of telling you to slow down. The traffic outside agrees. It is crawling.
sentences.zh: 下雨的星期一下午，是世界在告诉你慢下来。外面的车流表示同意。它正在爬行。

sentences.en: I make a second cup of tea. Chamomile this time. The first was green tea, but that was two hours ago.
sentences.zh: 我泡了第二杯茶。这次是洋甘菊。第一杯是绿茶，但那是两个小时前的事了。

sentences.en: My laptop screen dims to save power. I touch the trackpad to wake it up. The cursor blinks on an empty document.
sentences.zh: 笔记本电脑屏幕暗了省电。我碰了一下触控板唤醒它。光标在一个空白文档里闪烁。

sentences.en: Outside the window, a pigeon lands on the railing. It shakes water off its wings and stares at me through the glass.
sentences.zh: 窗外，一只鸽子落在栏杆上。它抖掉翅膀上的水，透过玻璃盯着我。

sentences.en: I should be working. But the rain and the pigeon and the second cup of tea have formed a coalition against productivity.
sentences.zh: 我应该在工作。但雨、鸽子和第二杯茶已经组成了一个反生产力联盟。

title: 雨中等出租车
coverQuote: Waiting for a taxi in the rain is a small exercise in hope. Every pair of headlights could be yours.

sentences.en: It is raining and I forgot my umbrella. I am standing under the narrow awning of a closed bakery.
sentences.zh: 下雨了，我忘了带伞。我站在一家关了门的面包店窄窄的雨棚下。

sentences.en: Waiting for a taxi in the rain is a small exercise in hope. Every pair of headlights could be yours. Most are not.
sentences.zh: 在雨中等出租车是一种小小的希望练习。每一对车灯都可能是你的。大多数不是。

sentences.en: I hold my phone out and try to book a ride. The app says: high demand. Estimated wait: 18 minutes.
sentences.zh: 我拿出手机试着叫车。软件显示：需求高峰。预计等待：18分钟。

sentences.en: A taxi splashes through a puddle three meters away. I step back too late. My shoes are wet now.
sentences.zh: 一辆出租车从三米外的水坑开过去溅起水花。我往后躲，已经晚了。鞋子湿了。

sentences.en: The bakery awning drips steadily onto my shoulder. I shift half a step to the left.
sentences.zh: 面包店雨棚上的水滴滴到我的肩膀上。我往左挪了半步。

sentences.en: Finally a green light on my phone. A car is coming. I step out from under the awning and wave at a pair of headlights.
sentences.zh: 终于手机上亮了一个绿灯。有车来了。我从雨棚下走出来，朝一对车灯挥手。

title: 便利店门口的雨天
coverQuote: The convenience store doorway is the unofficial shelter for people who forgot their umbrellas.

sentences.en: I step into the convenience store to buy an umbrella. They are sold out.
sentences.zh: 我走进便利店想买一把伞。卖完了。

sentences.en: So I buy a can of coffee and stand in the doorway, watching the rain. I am not the only one.
sentences.zh: 所以我买了一罐咖啡，站在门口看雨。我不是唯一一个这么做的。

sentences.en: The convenience store doorway is the unofficial shelter for people who forgot their umbrellas. We are a small, quiet club.
sentences.zh: 便利店门口是忘带伞的人的非官方避雨所。我们是一个小小的安静俱乐部。

sentences.en: A man in a suit runs past, holding a newspaper over his head. The newspaper is dissolving.
sentences.zh: 一个穿西装的男人跑过去，把报纸举在头上。报纸正在融化。

sentences.en: The rain slows down after ten minutes. One by one, the doorway club members leave. I stay until my coffee is finished.
sentences.zh: 十分钟后雨小了。门口俱乐部的人一个接一个走了。我喝完咖啡才走。

title: 雨天晾衣阳台
coverQuote: I forgot to bring the laundry in before the rain. Now my shirt is wetter than when I put it in the washer.

sentences.en: I hear the rain start and remember my laundry.
sentences.zh: 我听到雨声，想起了晾的衣服。

sentences.en: I run to the balcony. Too late.
sentences.zh: 我跑到阳台上。太晚了。

sentences.en: My shirt is wetter than when I put it in the washer.
sentences.zh: 我的衬衫比放进洗衣机的时候还湿。

sentences.en: I leave it there. It will dry again tomorrow.
sentences.zh: 我就让它挂在那。明天还会干的。

sentences.en: Rain on clean clothes smells different from rain on dirty ones. Cleaner.
sentences.zh: 干净衣服上的雨水和脏衣服上的味道不一样。更干净。

title: 地铁口的雨伞摊
coverQuote: As soon as the rain starts, the umbrella sellers appear at the subway exit like mushrooms after a storm.

sentences.en: The rain starts the moment I reach the top of the subway stairs. A perfect, inconvenient timing.
sentences.zh: 我刚走到地铁楼梯顶上，雨就下起来了。一个完美的、不合时宜的时间点。

sentences.en: As soon as the rain starts, the umbrella sellers appear at the subway exit like mushrooms after a storm. They unfold their wares on the wet ground.
sentences.zh: 雨一开始下，卖伞的就出现在地铁口，像暴风雨后的蘑菇。他们在湿地上摆开货品。

sentences.en: Ten yuan for a flimsy black umbrella. The kind that will break in the first strong wind. I buy one anyway.
sentences.zh: 十块钱一把薄薄的黑伞。那种第一次大风就会坏掉的。我还是买了一把。

sentences.en: The seller hands it to me without a word. No eye contact. He is already looking for the next customer.
sentences.zh: 卖伞的把伞递给我，没说话。没眼神接触。他已经在找下一个顾客了。

sentences.en: I open the umbrella and step into the rain. It works. For now, that is enough.
sentences.zh: 我撑开伞走进雨里。它撑住了。暂时来说，这就够了。

title: 秋雨夜的房间
coverQuote: The first autumn rain sounds different. It has more weight. Each drop lands like it means it.

sentences.en: The first autumn rain of the year arrives at 10pm. I know it is autumn rain because of the sound.
sentences.zh: 今年第一场秋雨在晚上十点来了。我知道是秋雨，因为声音不一样。

sentences.en: Autumn rain sounds heavier than summer rain. Each drop lands like it means it. The window frame vibrates slightly.
sentences.zh: 秋雨的声音比夏雨更沉。每一滴落下来都像是认真的。窗框在微微震动。

sentences.en: I pull my blanket up to my chin and leave the window open a crack. The cold air mixes with the warm room air.
sentences.zh: 我把毯子拉到下巴，窗户留了一条缝。冷空气和房间里的暖空气混在一起。

sentences.en: My cat jumps onto the bed and curls into a circle near my feet. She also knows it is autumn rain.
sentences.zh: 我的猫跳上床，在我脚边蜷成一团。她也知道这是秋雨。

sentences.en: I do not look at my phone. I just listen. The rain has a rhythm if you pay attention long enough.
sentences.zh: 我不看手机。我只是听着。如果你听的时间够长，雨是有节奏的。

title: 雨天车厢里的水渍
coverQuote: Everyone on the rainy day train is wet in a different place. Left shoulder. Right knee. Backpack. Hair.

sentences.en: The train on a rainy day smells different. Wet wool. Wet nylon. Wet newspaper.
sentences.zh: 雨天的地铁闻起来不一样。湿羊毛。湿尼龙。湿报纸。

sentences.en: Everyone on the rainy day train is wet in a different place. Left shoulder. Right knee. Backpack. Hair. No one is fully dry.
sentences.zh: 雨天车厢里每个人湿的地方都不一样。左肩。右膝。背包。头发。没有一个人是完全干的。

sentences.en: The floor of the car is tracked with muddy footprints. They crisscross in all directions, a map of everyone's morning.
sentences.zh: 车厢地板上踩满了泥脚印。它们交错纵横，是每个人早晨的地图。

sentences.en: A woman next to me wipes her glasses with a tissue. They fog up again immediately.
sentences.zh: 我旁边的女人用纸巾擦眼镜。马上又起雾了。

sentences.en: I hold the rail and feel someone else's wet sleeve brush against my hand. Neither of us apologizes. It is a rainy day. These things happen.
sentences.zh: 我扶着栏杆，别人的湿袖子擦过我的手。我们都没道歉。下雨天。这些事就是会发生的。

title: 撑着伞走路回家
coverQuote: Walking home under an umbrella, the world narrows to a small dry circle. Everything outside the circle is suggestion.

sentences.en: I decide to walk home instead of taking the bus. I have an umbrella and no reason to rush.
sentences.zh: 我决定走回家，不坐公交了。我有伞，也没有赶路的理由。

sentences.en: Walking home under an umbrella, the world narrows to a small dry circle. The circle moves with me. Everything outside is suggestion.
sentences.zh: 撑着伞走路回家，世界缩小成一个小小的干燥圆圈。这个圈随着我移动。圈外的一切都是暗示。

sentences.en: Headlights reflect off the wet asphalt in long yellow streaks. I step over them like puddles.
sentences.zh: 车灯在湿沥青上反射出长长的黄色光带。我像跨水坑一样跨过它们。

sentences.en: A couple passes me, sharing one umbrella. The taller one holds it. The shorter one holds the taller one's arm. Neither of them is dry.
sentences.zh: 一对情侣从我身边经过，共撑一把伞。高个子的撑伞。矮个子的挽着高个子的胳膊。两个人都没怎么遮到。

sentences.en: I take the long way. Past the closed flower shop. Past the 24-hour pharmacy. The umbrella makes a soft drumming sound the whole way.
sentences.zh: 我绕了远路。经过了打烊的花店。经过了24小时药店。一路上雨伞都在发出轻轻的鼓点声。

sentences.en: By the time I get home, my shoes are soaked but my head is dry. I count that as a win.
sentences.zh: 到家的时候鞋子湿透了，但头是干的。我把这算作一次胜利。

title: 书店门口避雨
coverQuote: I ducked into a bookstore to escape the rain and ended up staying for an hour. These things happen.

sentences.en: The rain caught me two blocks from home. I ducked into the nearest open door. It was a bookstore.
sentences.zh: 雨在离家两个街区的地方抓住了我。我钻进了最近的一扇开着的门。是一家书店。

sentences.en: The owner looked up from her newspaper and said: you can leave your wet umbrella in the bucket by the door.
sentences.zh: 店主从报纸上抬起头说：湿伞可以放在门边的桶里。

sentences.en: I did not plan to stay. But the rain got heavier. And the bookstore had a chair in the back corner.
sentences.zh: 我没打算待的。但雨越下越大。而且书店后角有一把椅子。

sentences.en: I picked up a book about birds. Not because I like birds. Because the cover was blue and the rain was gray.
sentences.zh: 我拿起一本关于鸟的书。不是因为喜欢鸟。是因为封面是蓝色的，而雨是灰色的。

sentences.en: An hour passed. The rain stopped. I bought the bird book. The owner wrapped it in a paper bag so it would not get wet.
sentences.zh: 一个小时过去了。雨停了。我买了那本鸟书。店主用纸袋包好，这样就不会湿。

title: 雨刷器
coverQuote: Windshield wipers are the metronome of a rainy drive. Back and forth. Back and forth. Keeping time with the rain.

sentences.en: I am in the passenger seat of my friend's car. The rain is steady now, not too heavy, not too light.
sentences.zh: 我坐在朋友车的副驾驶座上。雨势稳定了，不大不小。

sentences.en: The windshield wipers swing back and forth. Rubber on glass. A rhythmic squeak on the return stroke.
sentences.zh: 雨刷器来回摆动。橡胶刮过玻璃。每次回程都发出一声有节奏的吱嘎。

sentences.en: Windshield wipers are the metronome of a rainy drive. Back and forth. Back and forth. Keeping time with the rain.
sentences.zh: 雨刷器是雨天驾驶的节拍器。来。回。来。回。和雨同步。

sentences.en: The traffic lights ahead blur into red and green smears on the wet windshield. My friend turns down the music to concentrate.
sentences.zh: 前方的红绿灯在湿挡风玻璃上糊成红色和绿色的色块。朋友把音乐关小了，专心开车。

sentences.en: I trace a line on the fogged side window. It drips. I wipe it with my sleeve.
sentences.zh: 我在侧面雾窗上画了一条线。它淌下来了。我用袖子擦掉。

sentences.en: We drive without talking, just the wipers. It is not an awkward quiet. It is a rainy Tuesday quiet.
sentences.zh: 我们除了雨刷声什么也没说。不是尴尬的安静。就是下雨的周二安静。

title: 大雨前
coverQuote: The sky turned that specific shade of yellow-gray that means the rain is about to get serious.

sentences.en: I walked out of the office and looked up. The sky was wrong.
sentences.zh: 我走出办公室抬头一看。天空不对劲。

sentences.en: The sky turned that specific shade of yellow-gray that means the rain is about to get serious. The trees were already swaying.
sentences.zh: 天空变成那种特定的黄灰色，意思是雨要开始认真了。树已经在摇晃了。

sentences.en: People around me started walking faster. A woman ahead of me broke into a jog, holding her handbag over her head preemptively.
sentences.zh: 周围的人开始加快脚步。前面一个女人跑了起来，先把包举在头上。

sentences.en: I felt the first drop on my forearm. Then another on my cheek. Then the sky opened.
sentences.zh: 第一滴雨落在我的前臂上。然后又一滴在脸颊上。然后天开了。

sentences.en: I ducked under the awning of a bank. The rain hit the pavement so hard it bounced back up in a fine mist.
sentences.zh: 我钻到一家银行的雨棚下。雨砸在人行道上，弹回来变成一层细雾。

sentences.en: Five minutes later the street was empty. Just rain and me and a man in a bank doorway who had the same idea.
sentences.zh: 五分钟后街上就空了。只有雨、我和银行门口另一个跟我们想法一样的男的。

title: 午夜的雨
coverQuote: Midnight rain is better than daytime rain. No one expects you to do anything about it.

sentences.en: The rain started after midnight. I was already in bed.
sentences.zh: 雨在午夜之后开始下了。我已经在床上。

sentences.en: Midnight rain is better than daytime rain. No one expects you to go out in it. You can just listen.
sentences.zh: 午夜的雨比白天的雨好。没人指望你在这种天气出门。你只需要听。

sentences.en: I opened my window a little. The sound came in clearer. Rain on the roof. Rain in the gutters. Rain on the leaves of the plant on my windowsill.
sentences.zh: 我把窗户开了一小条缝。雨声更清楚了。打在屋顶上的。落在排水沟里的。滴在窗台植物叶子上的。

sentences.en: I did not check my phone. I did not turn on the light. I just lay there in the dark with the rain.
sentences.zh: 我没看手机。没开灯。就躺在黑暗中和雨一起。

sentences.en: At some point I fell asleep. The rain was still going. It probably continued all night.
sentences.zh: 不知什么时候我睡着了。雨还在下。大概下了一整夜。

title: 雨滴在窗上的比赛
coverQuote: Two raindrops on the window. They race to the bottom. The one on the left wins. I am the only spectator.

sentences.en: I sit by the window. It is raining outside.
sentences.zh: 我坐在窗边。外面在下雨。

sentences.en: Two raindrops on the glass are racing.
sentences.zh: 玻璃上有两颗雨滴在比赛。

sentences.en: The left one moves fast. The right one stops.
sentences.zh: 左边那颗跑得快。右边那颗停住了。

sentences.en: The left drop wins. It reaches the bottom first.
sentences.zh: 左边的赢了。它先到了底下。

sentences.en: I wait for the next race. Two new drops appear.
sentences.zh: 我等着下一场比赛。两颗新的雨滴出现了。

title: 早高峰地铁的沉默
coverQuote: In the morning rush, a packed train car can be completely silent. Fifty people, zero words.

sentences.en: The 8:15 train is packed as always. I squeeze in and find a spot near the door.
sentences.zh: 八点一刻的车照常爆满。我挤进去，在门边找了个地方。

sentences.en: In the morning rush, a packed train car can be completely silent. Fifty people pressed together, zero words spoken.
sentences.zh: 早高峰，一车厢挤满的人可以完全沉默。五十个人挤在一起，零个字。

sentences.en: A man next to me is reading a novel on his phone. The font is tiny. He scrolls with his thumb every twenty seconds.
sentences.zh: 我旁边一个男的正在手机上看小说。字号非常小。他每二十秒用拇指滑一次。

sentences.en: Someone's backpack is digging into my shoulder. I shift slightly. The backpack shifts too.
sentences.zh: 有人的背包正顶着我的肩膀。我微微动了一下。那个背包也跟着动了。

sentences.en: The train announcement crackles: next station. Half the car shuffles toward the doors. The other half spreads out into the new space.
sentences.zh: 广播噼里啪啦响：下一站。半车厢人挪向门口。另一半人散开，占满腾出的空间。

sentences.en: At my stop I push through the crowd and step onto the platform. The silence is replaced by the echo of footsteps.
sentences.zh: 到站了我挤过人群，踏上站台。沉默被脚步声的回音取代。

title: 写字楼大厅的傍晚
coverQuote: Office lobbies at 6pm all smell the same. Floor cleaner and exhaustion.

sentences.en: The elevator doors open onto the ground floor lobby. A stream of people pours out, each one pulling out their phone.
sentences.zh: 电梯门在一楼大厅打开。人流涌出来，每个人都在掏手机。

sentences.en: Office lobbies at 6pm all smell the same. Floor cleaner. Exhaustion. The faint metallic tang of too many elevator rides.
sentences.zh: 傍晚六点的写字楼大厅闻起来都一样。地板清洁剂。疲惫。太多趟电梯留下的淡淡金属味。

sentences.en: The security guard at the front desk is packing up his dinner container. He wipes the desk with a tissue.
sentences.zh: 前台保安正在收拾他的饭盒。他用纸巾擦了擦桌子。

sentences.en: I push through the revolving door. The outside air hits my face. It is the best thing I have felt all day.
sentences.zh: 我推着旋转门走出去。外面的空气打在脸上。这是一整天里我感受到的最好的东西。

sentences.en: I stand on the sidewalk for a minute, deciding which way to go. Home is left. The bar is right. I go left.
sentences.zh: 我在人行道上站了一分钟，决定往哪走。家在左边。酒吧在右边。我往左走了。

title: 十字路口等红灯
coverQuote: At a busy intersection during rush hour, you can learn everything about a city by watching who crosses first.

sentences.en: I stop at the crosswalk. The red hand is blinking. Cars rush past in both directions.
sentences.zh: 我在斑马线前停下来。红灯的小红人在闪。车在两个方向呼啸而过。

sentences.en: Around me, people check their phones. Adjust their bags. Shift their weight from foot to foot.
sentences.zh: 我周围，人们在看手机。调整背包。重心从左脚换到右脚。

sentences.en: A delivery scooter runs the red light. The driver leans into the turn. A taxi honks.
sentences.zh: 一辆外卖电动车闯了红灯。骑手压着弯转过去。一辆出租车按了喇叭。

sentences.en: The light changes. The crowd surges forward. A woman in heels walks fast. An old man takes his time. Everyone moves at their own speed in the same crossing.
sentences.zh: 灯变了。人群涌向前。一个穿高跟鞋的女人走得飞快。一个老人慢悠悠的。同一个路口，每个人都有自己的速度。

sentences.en: I reach the other side and keep walking. Behind me the light changes back to red. The next crowd gathers.
sentences.zh: 我走到对面继续走。身后灯又变红了。下一群人开始聚集。

title: 城市清晨的清洁工
coverQuote: The street cleaners start at 5am. By the time the city wakes up, they have already erased yesterday.

sentences.en: I am walking home at 5am for reasons I will not explain. The sky is that pale gray that comes before sunrise.
sentences.zh: 我凌晨五点走回家，原因就不解释了。天空是日出前那种淡灰色。

sentences.en: The street cleaners are already out. Two of them in orange vests, pushing wide brooms along the gutter. A third follows with a hose.
sentences.zh: 清洁工已经出来了。两个穿着橙色背心的工人推着大扫帚沿着排水沟走。第三个人拿着水管跟在后面。

sentences.en: The street cleaners start at 5am. By the time the city wakes up, they have already erased yesterday. The cigarette butts, the lottery tickets, the receipts.
sentences.zh: 清洁工凌晨五点就开始了。等城市醒来，他们已经擦掉了昨天。烟头、彩票、小票。

sentences.en: One of them looks up as I pass. I nod. He nods back. The hose keeps running.
sentences.zh: 我经过时其中一个人抬头看了一眼。我点了点头。他回点了一下。水管一直在流。

sentences.en: I walk past the wet pavement they have just cleaned. It gleams under the streetlight. For a few hours, this street is new.
sentences.zh: 我走过他们刚清洗过的湿路面。它在路灯下闪着光。有几个小时，这条街是全新的。

title: 写字楼咖啡机前
coverQuote: The office coffee machine is where you go when you need a break from your screen and do not want to go outside.

sentences.en: It is 3pm and my eyes hurt from the screen. I push back my chair and walk to the pantry.
sentences.zh: 下午三点，眼睛被屏幕刺得发疼。我推开椅子走向茶水间。

sentences.en: The office coffee machine hums in the corner. Someone has left a mug next to it with a tea bag still inside.
sentences.zh: 办公室的咖啡机在角落里嗡嗡响。有人留了一个杯子在旁边，茶包还在里面。

sentences.en: The office coffee machine is where you go when you need a break from your screen and do not want to go outside. It is the unofficial break room within the break room.
sentences.zh: 办公室咖啡机是你需要离开屏幕但又不想出去的时候去的地方。它是休息室里的非官方休息室。

sentences.en: I press the button for an Americano. The machine grinds the beans loudly. For ten seconds I cannot hear my own thoughts.
sentences.zh: 我按了美式的按钮。机器大声磨豆。十秒钟内我听不到自己的想法。

sentences.en: A colleague walks in, sees me, sighs, and says: long day. I say: yeah. That is the whole conversation. It is enough.
sentences.zh: 一个同事走进来，看到我，叹了口气说：今天好长。我说：是啊。整段对话就这些。够了。

sentences.en: I take my coffee back to my desk. It is slightly too bitter. I drink it anyway.
sentences.zh: 我端着咖啡回到工位。稍微苦了点。我还是喝了。

title: 快节奏城市的慢书店
coverQuote: The used bookstore on Fuzhou Road has been there since 1998. The city grew up around it. It did not change.

sentences.en: The used bookstore on Fuzhou Road is wedged between a bubble tea shop and a phone repair kiosk.
sentences.zh: 福州路上的旧书店夹在一家奶茶店和一个修手机的摊位之间。

sentences.en: It has been there since 1998. The city grew up around it. Skyscrapers rose. Subway lines opened. The bookstore did not change.
sentences.zh: 它从1998年就在那儿了。城市在它周围长高。摩天大楼竖起来。地铁线开通了。书店没变。

sentences.en: The owner is a man in his sixties. He wears reading glasses on a chain around his neck. He prices books with a pencil.
sentences.zh: 店主是个六十多岁的男人。他戴着一副挂在脖子上的老花镜。他用铅笔给书标价。

sentences.en: The shelves are organized by a system only he understands. Poetry next to cookbooks. History next to sci-fi. It works if you do not think too hard about it.
sentences.zh: 书架按照只有他懂的系统排列。诗歌旁边是菜谱。历史旁边是科幻。如果你不去想它，它是合理的。

sentences.en: I find a thin volume of essays from the 1980s. The pages are yellow. It smells like a used bookstore should.
sentences.zh: 我找到一本八十年代的薄薄的散文集。书页泛黄了。闻起来就是一个旧书店该有的味道。

sentences.en: I pay the owner. He wraps the book in a piece of brown paper and hands it to me. Come back when you finish that one, he says.
sentences.zh: 我付钱给店主。他用一张牛皮纸把书包好递给我。"那本看完了再来。"他说。

title: 公司楼下的午餐
coverQuote: I eat the same rice bowl from the same shop every Tuesday. The owner starts making it when she sees me walk in.

sentences.en: Every Tuesday I eat lunch at the small shop under my office.
sentences.zh: 每周二我都在公司楼下的小店吃午餐。

sentences.en: The owner knows my order. A rice bowl with egg and pork.
sentences.zh: 老板知道我要什么。一碗鸡蛋猪肉饭。

sentences.en: She starts making it when she sees me walk in.
sentences.zh: 她看到我进门就开始做了。

sentences.en: I sit at the same table. The table has a small scratch on the left side.
sentences.zh: 我坐在同一张桌子前。桌子左边有一道小小的划痕。

sentences.en: The food comes. I eat. I pay. I leave. Every Tuesday.
sentences.zh: 饭来了。我吃。我付钱。我走了。每个周二。

title: 末班电梯里的人
coverQuote: The last elevator down from the office carries a specific kind of tired. The kind that has been building since 9am.

sentences.en: I press the down button at 9:47pm. The elevator arrives empty.
sentences.zh: 晚上九点四十七分我按下行按钮。电梯到了，里面是空的。

sentences.en: Three floors down, it stops. A woman in a gray cardigan gets in. She is holding a half-empty cup of tea.
sentences.zh: 下了三层，电梯停了。一个穿灰色开衫的女人进来。她端着一杯喝了一半的茶。

sentences.en: We stand in opposite corners. The elevator music is a soft jazz cover of a pop song. Neither of us comments on it.
sentences.zh: 我们站在电梯对角。电梯里放着软爵士版的流行歌。我们都没说什么。

sentences.en: The elevator descends. Floor numbers tick down. 18. 15. 12. The woman rubs her eyes with the back of her hand.
sentences.zh: 电梯下行。楼层数往下跳。18。15。12。那个女人用手背揉了揉眼睛。

sentences.en: Lobby. The doors open. She goes left. I go right. We will probably do this again tomorrow.
sentences.zh: 大厅。门开了。她往左走了。我往右走了。我们明天大概还会这样。

title: 快递驿站
coverQuote: The package pickup point is a small room behind a fruit shop. The owner knows everyone by their phone number.

sentences.en: I got a notification on my phone: your package has arrived. I walk to the pickup point behind the fruit shop.
sentences.zh: 手机上收到通知：您的包裹已到。我走向水果店后面的快递驿站。

sentences.en: The owner is a woman in her forties. She is eating sunflower seeds and watching a drama on a small TV.
sentences.zh: 店主是个四十多岁的女人。她在磕瓜子，看着一台小电视上的电视剧。

sentences.en: She asks for the last four digits of my phone number. I tell her. She disappears into the shelves and comes back with a brown box.
sentences.zh: 她问我手机尾号。我告诉了她。她消失在货架间，拿着一个棕色盒子回来了。

sentences.en: She knows everyone by their phone number. The tall man with the cat food. The girl who orders books. The guy who always returns things.
sentences.zh: 她凭手机号认识每个人。买猫粮的高个子男的。订书的女生。老是退货的那个男的。

sentences.en: I take my box and leave. On the way out I pass a man carrying a bag of oranges from the fruit shop. We nod at each other.
sentences.zh: 我拿着盒子走了。出门时经过一个提着水果店橙子的男人。我们互相点了点头。

title: 报亭
coverQuote: The newsstand on the corner still opens every morning at 7. The old man who runs it has been there since before I was born.

sentences.en: There is a newsstand on the corner of my street. It sells newspapers, magazines, bottled water, and phone chargers.
sentences.zh: 我住的那条街角有一个报亭。卖报纸、杂志、矿泉水和手机充电线。

sentences.en: The old man who runs it opens every morning at 7am. Rain or shine. He arranges the newspapers in neat rows.
sentences.zh: 开报亭的老人每天早上七点开门。风雨无阻。他把报纸排成整整齐齐的几排。

sentences.en: He has been there since before I was born. He watched this street change from low buildings to high-rises.
sentences.zh: 我还没出生他就在那了。他看着这条街从矮楼变成高楼。

sentences.en: I stop to buy a bottle of water. He takes my coins and hands me the water. His hands are steady.
sentences.zh: 我停下来买一瓶水。他接过硬币，把水递给我。他的手很稳。

sentences.en: Hot today, he says. I say yes. That is our conversation. We have it every time.
sentences.zh: "今天热。"他说。我说"是啊。"这是我们的对话。每次都是这样。

sentences.en: I wonder how much longer the newsstand will be there. I do not ask. I just buy my water and say thank you.
sentences.zh: 我不知道报亭还能在那多久。我没问。我只是买了水，道了谢。

title: 医院走廊的自动贩卖机
coverQuote: Hospital vending machines do not care why you are there. They just hum and wait for your coins.

sentences.en: The hospital corridor at 11pm is a different kind of quiet. Not peaceful. Just waiting.
sentences.zh: 晚上十一点的医院走廊是一种不同的安静。不是平静。只是在等待。

sentences.en: I am here because someone I know is in room 412. I needed air. I found the vending machine alcove instead.
sentences.zh: 我在这是因为我认识的某个人在412病房。我需要透口气。结果找到了自动贩卖机的小隔间。

sentences.en: Hospital vending machines do not care why you are there. They just hum and wait for your coins. This one has seen more tears than a therapist.
sentences.zh: 医院的自动贩卖机不关心你为什么在这。它们只是嗡嗡响，等你的硬币。这台机器见过比心理医生还多的眼泪。

sentences.en: I buy a can of warm milk tea. It clunks into the tray. The sound echoes down the empty corridor.
sentences.zh: 我买了一罐温奶茶。它咣当掉进托盘里。声音在空荡荡的走廊里回荡。

sentences.en: The can is warm in my hands. I hold it for a while before opening it. Sometimes just holding something warm is enough.
sentences.zh: 罐子在手里暖暖的。我握了一会儿才打开。有时候只是握着一样暖和的东西就够了。

title: 出租车司机的晚间广播
coverQuote: The taxi driver is listening to a late-night radio show. The host is taking calls from people who cannot sleep.

sentences.en: I get into a taxi after a long dinner with old friends. The driver is listening to the radio.
sentences.zh: 和老朋友吃了顿很长的晚饭后我上了一辆出租车。司机在听广播。

sentences.en: The radio host has a low, calm voice. He is taking calls from people who cannot sleep. A woman calls in. She says she quit her job today.
sentences.zh: 电台主持人的声音低沉平静。他在接睡不着的人打来的电话。一个女人打进来。她说她今天辞职了。

sentences.en: The taxi driver turns the volume up slightly. He does not say anything. He just drives and listens.
sentences.zh: 出租车司机把音量稍微调大了一点。他什么也没说。只是开着车，听着。

sentences.en: The caller talks about her boss. Her long commute. The last straw was a broken printer. The driver shakes his head slowly.
sentences.zh: 打进电话的人讲她的老板。她漫长的通勤。最后一根稻草是一台坏了的打印机。司机缓缓摇了摇头。

sentences.en: We stop at a red light. The driver finally speaks: three more years, he says. Then I retire. The light turns green. We drive on.
sentences.zh: 我们在红灯前停下来。司机终于开口了："再三年，"他说，"然后我就退休了。"灯变绿了。我们继续开。

title: 天桥上看车流
coverQuote: Standing on a pedestrian bridge watching cars flow underneath is the closest a city gets to a river.

sentences.en: I stop halfway across the pedestrian bridge. No one is behind me. I lean on the railing.
sentences.zh: 我走天桥走了一半停了下来。身后没人。我靠在栏杆上。

sentences.en: Below me, six lanes of traffic flow in two directions. Headlights white. Taillights red. A river of light.
sentences.zh: 下面，六条车道向两个方向流动。前灯是白色的。尾灯是红色的。一条光的河。

sentences.en: Standing on a pedestrian bridge watching cars flow underneath is the closest a city gets to a river. The current never stops.
sentences.zh: 站在天桥上看车流从下面经过，是一座城市最接近一条河的样子。水流永不停歇。

sentences.en: A woman walks past me with a shopping bag. She does not stop. She has somewhere to be.
sentences.zh: 一个女人提着购物袋从我身边经过。她没有停。她有地方要去。

sentences.en: I stay for five more minutes. The cars keep coming. The river keeps flowing. Eventually I walk on.
sentences.zh: 我又站了五分钟。车流不断。河水不停。最终我继续走了。

title: 街角的早餐摊
coverQuote: The breakfast stall on the corner opens at 6am. By 9am it is gone. If you do not know it is there, you miss it.

sentences.en: There is a breakfast stall on the corner near my apartment. It opens at 6am.
sentences.zh: 我公寓附近的街角有一个早餐摊。早上六点开门。

sentences.en: The woman who runs it makes jianbing. One hand cracks the egg, the other spreads the batter. She has been doing this for sixteen years.
sentences.zh: 摊主是个做煎饼的女人。一只手打蛋，另一只手抹面糊。她已经这样做了十六年。

sentences.en: By 9am the stall is gone. The cart folds up and rolls away. If you do not know it is there, you miss it.
sentences.zh: 早上九点，摊子就消失了。推车折叠起来推走了。如果你不知道它在哪，你就会错过。

sentences.en: I go every Saturday. She knows my order: one egg, no cilantro, extra chili.
sentences.zh: 我每周六都去。她知道我要什么：一个蛋，不要香菜，多辣。

sentences.en: This Saturday the stall is not there. The corner is just a corner again. I stand there for a minute with my hands in my pockets.
sentences.zh: 这个周六摊子没来。街角又变回了普通的街角。我手插在口袋里在那儿站了一分钟。

title: 地下通道的吉他手
coverQuote: The man in the underground passage plays the same three songs every night. I know the order now. First the slow one, then the fast one, then the sad one.

sentences.en: Every night around 9pm, a man with a guitar sits down in the underground passage between Line 1 and Line 2.
sentences.zh: 每天晚上九点左右，一个带着吉他的男人在一号线和二号线之间的地下通道坐下来。

sentences.en: He plays the same three songs every night. I know the order now. First the slow one. Then the fast one. Then the sad one.
sentences.zh: 他每晚弹同样的三首歌。我现在知道顺序了。先是慢的那首。然后是快的那首。然后是伤心的那首。

sentences.en: His guitar case is open in front of him. There are a few coins inside. A five-yuan note. A pack of tissues someone left.
sentences.zh: 他的吉他盒在面前打开着。里面有几枚硬币。一张五块钱。一包谁留下的纸巾。

sentences.en: I drop a coin in as I walk past. He does not look up. He is in the middle of the sad song. His fingers move on the strings like they belong there.
sentences.zh: 我经过时丢了一个硬币进去。他没抬头。他正在弹伤心的那首。他的手指在弦上移动，好像它们本来就该在那。

sentences.en: I slow down as I walk past. I always do. The sad song follows me up the stairs and into the night.
sentences.zh: 我经过时刻意放慢了脚步。每次都是。伤心的歌跟着我上了楼梯，进入夜色中。

title: 城建围挡后的路
coverQuote: The city is always under construction. The familiar path becomes unfamiliar overnight. A new wall. A detour sign.

sentences.en: I walked the same route to work for two years. I knew every crack in the pavement.
sentences.zh: 我走同一条路去上班走了两年。我认识人行道上的每一道裂缝。

sentences.en: This morning there was a blue construction wall where the old bakery used to be. The bakery closed six months ago. Now even the building is gone.
sentences.zh: 今天早上，老面包店原来的地方竖起了一道蓝色施工围挡。面包店六个月前就关了。现在连那栋楼都没了。

sentences.en: The city is always under construction. The familiar becomes unfamiliar overnight. A new wall. A detour sign. You learn to adapt.
sentences.zh: 这座城市永远在施工中。熟悉的东西一夜之间变陌生。一道新墙。一块绕行指示牌。你学会了适应。

sentences.en: I had to walk an extra block to get around it. The detour took me past a noodle shop I had never noticed before.
sentences.zh: 我不得不绕一个街区才能过去。绕路经过了一家我从来注意到的面馆。

sentences.en: Maybe I will try the noodle shop tomorrow. The old bakery is not coming back. But the noodle shop smells good.
sentences.zh: 也许明天我会去那家面馆试试。老面包店回不来了。但面馆闻起来很香。

title: 公园长椅上的十分钟
coverQuote: I sit on a park bench and watch a leaf fall. It takes longer than you would think.

sentences.en: I found a bench near the pond. It was empty.
sentences.zh: 我在池塘边找到一张长椅。是空的。

sentences.en: I sat down. No phone. No book. Just sitting.
sentences.zh: 我坐下来。没带手机。没带书。就是坐着。

sentences.en: A leaf fell from the tree above. It took a long time to reach the ground.
sentences.zh: 一片叶子从头顶的树上落下来。它用了很久才到地面。

sentences.en: An old man walked past with a small dog. The dog stopped to look at me.
sentences.zh: 一个老人带着一只小狗走过去。小狗停下来看了看我。

sentences.en: I stayed for ten minutes. That is longer than I have done nothing in a while.
sentences.zh: 我待了十分钟。比我很久以来什么都不做的时间都长。

title: 公园里喂猫的人
coverQuote: Every evening at 6pm, a woman comes to the park with a paper bag. The cats know her.

sentences.en: I see her every evening. She arrives at 6pm with a crumpled paper bag.
sentences.zh: 我每晚都看到她。她六点到，拿着一个皱皱的纸袋。

sentences.en: Three cats appear from behind the bushes. A gray one. An orange one. A black one with a white paw.
sentences.zh: 三只猫从灌木丛后面出来。一只灰的。一只橘的。一只黑猫有一只白爪子。

sentences.en: She pours dry food into a small plastic bowl. The cats eat in a neat row, like commas on a page.
sentences.zh: 她把猫粮倒进一个小塑料碗里。三只猫排成一排吃，像纸上的逗号。

sentences.en: She does not pet them. She just watches with her hands in her pockets.
sentences.zh: 她不摸它们。她只是手插在兜里看着。

sentences.en: When the food is gone, the cats leave one by one. The woman picks up the bowl and walks away.
sentences.zh: 吃完了，猫一只一只走了。女人拿起碗，走开了。

sentences.en: I have never spoken to her. I do not know her name. But at 6pm, we are both in the park.
sentences.zh: 我从来没跟她说过话。我不知道她的名字。但下午六点，我们都在公园里。

title: 深夜房间听播客
coverQuote: I lie in the dark with one earbud in, listening to someone talk about history. Outside, a distant siren.

sentences.en: I put in one earbud and press play. A voice starts talking about ancient Rome.
sentences.zh: 我塞了一只耳机，按下播放。一个声音开始讲古罗马。

sentences.en: I do not care about ancient Rome. But the voice is calm and the room is dark.
sentences.zh: 我不在乎古罗马。但那个声音很平静，房间很暗。

sentences.en: I lie on my back with one earbud in. Half listening. Half drifting.
sentences.zh: 我仰面躺着，戴着一只耳机。半听半飘。

sentences.en: Outside, a distant siren passes. The voice keeps talking about aqueducts.
sentences.zh: 外面，远处传来一阵警笛声。那个声音继续讲着引水渠。

sentences.en: The episode ends. The next one auto-plays. I do not turn it off.
sentences.zh: 这集结束了。下一集自动播放。我没关。

sentences.en: At some point I fall asleep. In the morning the earbud is tangled in my hair.
sentences.zh: 不知什么时候我睡着了。早上耳机缠在头发里。

title: 车窗外的雨夜
coverQuote: The backseat of a taxi on a rainy night is a small dry world. Outside, the city dissolves.

sentences.en: I get into a taxi just as the rain gets heavier. Good timing for once.
sentences.zh: 我刚坐进出租车雨就下大了。难得的好时机。

sentences.en: The backseat of a taxi on a rainy night is a small dry world. Outside, the city dissolves into streaks of color.
sentences.zh: 雨夜里出租车后座是一个小小的干燥世界。外面，城市溶化成彩色的条纹。

sentences.en: The driver hums along to an old song on the radio. His voice is slightly off-key. I do not mind.
sentences.zh: 司机跟着广播里一首老歌哼着。他有点跑调。我不介意。

sentences.en: We pass a brightly lit hot pot restaurant. Steam on the windows. People laughing inside. A frame of warmth in the wet dark.
sentences.zh: 我们经过一家灯火通明的火锅店。窗户上都是蒸汽。里面的人在笑。湿冷的黑夜里一框暖意。

sentences.en: The meter ticks up. The wipers swing. The driver hums. I lean my head against the window and watch.
sentences.zh: 计价器在跳。雨刷在摆。司机在哼歌。我靠着车窗看着。

title: 凌晨三点醒来喝水
coverQuote: Waking up at 3am to drink water is a small ritual. You stand in the dark kitchen and for a moment, the world is just you and the glass.

sentences.en: I wake up at 3:14am. The red numbers on the clock confirm it.
sentences.zh: 凌晨三点十四分我醒了。钟上的红色数字确认了这一点。

sentences.en: My mouth is dry. I slide out of bed and walk to the kitchen without turning on lights.
sentences.zh: 嘴里很干。我滑下床，摸黑走到厨房。

sentences.en: I pour water from the pitcher. The glug-glug sound is loud in the silent kitchen.
sentences.zh: 我从凉水壶里倒水。咕嘟咕嘟的声音在安静的厨房里很响。

sentences.en: I stand by the sink and drink. For a moment the world is just me and the glass.
sentences.zh: 我站在水槽边喝水。有那么一会儿世界只有我和这个杯子。

sentences.en: I rinse the glass, leave it on the rack, and go back to bed. The whole thing took two minutes.
sentences.zh: 我冲了杯子，放在沥水架上，回到床上。整个过程花了两分钟。

title: 周日黄昏的街道
coverQuote: Sunday evening streets have a specific kind of quiet. The weekend is over but Monday has not started yet.

sentences.en: I go for a walk on Sunday evening. The streets are quieter than usual.
sentences.zh: 周日傍晚我出去散步。街道比平时安静。

sentences.en: Sunday evening has a specific kind of quiet. The weekend is over but Monday has not started yet. A gap.
sentences.zh: 周日的傍晚有一种特定的安静。周末结束了，周一还没开始。一个缝隙。

sentences.en: A father teaches his daughter to ride a bike in the empty parking lot. She wobbles. He cheers.
sentences.zh: 一个爸爸在空停车场教女儿骑车。她摇摇晃晃的。他在喊加油。

sentences.en: The convenience store lights flicker on. The streetlights follow a few minutes later.
sentences.zh: 便利店的灯亮了。几分钟后路灯也跟着亮了。

sentences.en: I walk until the sky is completely dark. Then I turn around and walk home.
sentences.zh: 我一直走到天完全黑了。然后转身走回家。

title: 地铁站台上的学生
coverQuote: A girl in school uniform sits on the platform bench, highlighting her textbook in neon yellow. The train comes. She does not look up.

sentences.en: The platform is crowded at 5pm. Students in uniforms. Office workers with loosened ties.
sentences.zh: 下午五点的站台挤满了人。穿校服的学生。松了领带的上班族。

sentences.en: A girl sits on the bench, bent over a textbook. She highlights a line in neon yellow.
sentences.zh: 一个女孩坐在长椅上，埋头看课本。她用荧光黄划了一行。

sentences.en: The train arrives with a rush of wind. People push forward. She does not look up.
sentences.zh: 列车带着一阵风进站。人们往前挤。她没有抬头。

sentences.en: She turns a page. Highlights another line. The train doors close and leave without her.
sentences.zh: 她翻了一页。又划了一行。列车门关了，没带她就走了。

sentences.en: The next train will come in four minutes. She has four more minutes of studying.
sentences.zh: 下一趟车四分钟后到。她还有四分钟可以学习。
